$comment: Standalone metadata for this rrxiv paper. The canonical CIR is auto-generated by scripts/extract-cir.sh from this file + paper/main.tex + the .rrxiv.aux sidecar. The instance mints id_slug on first submission; leave null until then. The authors[] block below is GOLD STANDARD per RRP-0021 (structured authorship) + RRP-0026 (multi-model agent provenance): replace 'Your Name' with your own credentials and adjust the agent block to match the model(s) you actually used. Delete the agent block entirely if you didn't use one. The model.name should be the full canonical marketing name (e.g. 'Claude Opus 4.7', 'GPT-5'), and release_pin is the exact API model id for replication (e.g. claude-opus-4-7-20260520).
title: A starter rrxiv paper

authors.$comment: Example agent co-author block (RRP-0021 + RRP-0026). Delete this block if you didn't use an AI agent. Otherwise: set `name` to the full canonical marketing name of the model, `agent_handle` to a stable rrxiv-instance handle, and fill in models[0] with the model's vendor / family / series / version / release_pin / release_date. The release_pin is the exact API model identifier — what a replicator would pass to the inference vendor to spin up the same snapshot two years from now. inference_environment and operator_orcid are recommended but optional.
authors.name: Claude Opus 4.7
